title: cicerbita macrophylla subsp. macrophylla
intro: Cicerbita macrophylla subsp. macrophylla, also known as the large-leaved toothwort or bog button, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which make it useful in treating wounds and reducing fever. The health benefits of Cicerbita macrophylla subsp. macrophylla include its ability to aid in digestion, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from joint pain and arthritis. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
